groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Minimum inputs for a Facebook-ready draft.

groups.title: Post information
groups.description: Type, message, length, tone, and schedule context.

groups.title: Publishing context
groups.description: Page, Group, Event, or profile — and what features exist.

groups.title: Organization / creator
groups.description: Real brand or creator details only.

groups.title: Audience
groups.description: Who sees this and what they need.

groups.title: Product / service
groups.description: Current stage and real features — no vaporware.

groups.title: Offer
groups.description: Prices, codes, and deadlines only if confirmed.

groups.title: Event
groups.description: Absolute dates and real event details.

groups.title: Personal story
groups.description: Only approved experiences — never invent “I…” stories.

groups.title: Media
groups.description: Images, video, rights, and alt text needs.

groups.title: AI / synthetic media
groups.description: Disclosure when synthetic media is used.

groups.title: Corrections & updates
groups.description: What is being corrected and why.

groups.title: A/B testing
groups.description: One variable at a time if testing.

groups.title: Organic vs paid
groups.description: Keep organic copy honest when ads are separate.

variables.label: Topic
variables.placeholder: How we ship content with templates as a small team
variables.hint: What the post is about — specific to Facebook context.

help.what: Post topic.
help.why: Keeps the Facebook post focused on one idea.
help.example: Announcing a new free Blog Article template
help.avoid: Generic multi-platform blurbs or several unrelated topics.

variables.label: Post Type
variables.hint: Pick one confirmed type — do not switch just to sell harder.

help.what: Confirmed Facebook post type.
help.why: Structure and CTA depend on type (update vs offer vs discussion).
help.example: educational post
help.avoid: Turning every post into a promotional post without basis.

variables.label: Audience
variables.placeholder: small-business owners and marketers who use Facebook
variables.hint: Primary Facebook audience for this post.

help.what: Audience — a parameter from the Facebook Post brief (Audience).
help.why: When provided, the model uses this instead of inventing facts, offers, stories, or engagement tricks. Empty fields stay unset ([audience]).
help.example: small-business owners and marketers who use Facebook
help.avoid: Invented social proof, fake urgency, unverified product claims, or private details for audience.
